mechanism_narrative: A downward path in FEDFUNDS lowers the discount rate on future earnings and improves equity valuation over the next month; the signal is positive when policy is easing and fails when rates are flat or rising.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are not positive in a majority of months where FEDFUNDS is falling on a 3-month basis, or if rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE marks labor-market deterioration and rising recession probability, which compresses risk appetite and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations deliver positive 20-day SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the sign of the average forward return is positive after unemployment shocks.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent improvement in RSAFS and HOUST indicates stabilizing household demand and a housing-led recovery, which supports positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if joint RSAFS and HOUST improvements do not outperform either series alone, or if positive 60-day SPX returns fail to occur in at least 55% of joint-improvement cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO while PAYEMS remains weak signals output is recovering faster than labor income, which often precedes cyclical equity upside over the next month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises above its 3-month moving average while PAYEMS remains below its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition does not produce positive 20-day returns more often than not, or if the interaction term adds no predictive value relative to INDPRO alone.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL is accelerating while FEDFUNDS remains near zero, real policy rates fall and inflation expectations dominate, which should support a short-term equity rebound as financial conditions stay loose.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation rises above FEDFUNDS by more than 2pp
failure_condition: Reject if crossing the inflation-minus-policy threshold is not followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in at least 55% of cases, or if returns are systematically negative after the trigger.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in GDPC1 identifies a broad demand recession and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter as earnings expectations are revised down.
failure_condition: Reject if GDP contraction quarters are not followed by negative 60-day SPX returns on average, or if the sign of the effect is positive across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in PAYEMS and rise in UNRATE signals a true labor-market deterioration, not just noise, and should pressure equities lower over the next month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS falls and UNRATE rises in the same month
failure_condition: Reject if the combined PAYEMS-down/UNRATE-up condition fails to produce negative 20-day returns more often than 55%, or if either series alone explains the effect fully.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in HOUST after a prior collapse signals stabilization in interest-sensitive demand and construction activity, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises by at least 5% from its prior 3-month low
failure_condition: Reject if rebound events in HOUST do not precede positive 5-day SPX returns in a majority of cases, or if the average return after the trigger is not above baseline.
